title: hieracium doronicifolium subsp. scabiosifolium
intro: Hieracium doronicifolium subsp. scabiosifolium, also known as Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species within the Asteraceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and jagged, toothed leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are thought to be beneficial in treating various health conditions, including fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Hieracium doronicifolium subsp. scabiosifolium are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, which are believed to have antioxidant and immune-boosting effects. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
